Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to analyze a provided Procure to Pay P2P event log identify anomalies deviations from standard best practices and explain why they are problematic

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Standard P2P Process Before looking at the data I need to establish a baseline of what a normal or ideal P2P process looks like Standard Sequence Requisition Create Purchase Requisition PR Approval Approve PR Internal control Ordering Issue Purchase Order PO Receipt Receive Goods Services Invoice Vendor Invoice Received Matching Match Invoice to PO and often GRN Payment Release Payment Closure Close Case Key Controls Logic Requisition must happen before PO Approval must happen before PO usually PO must happen before Invoice and before Goods Receipt usually to ensure authorization Goods Receipt usually happens before or around Invoice to verify quantity quality Invoice Matching usually happens after Invoice and after PO and often after Goods Receipt Payment must happen after Matching to ensure validity Payment must happen before Case Close Time gaps should make sense for example payment before invoice is impossible

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1001 Sequence Create PR Approve PR Issue PO Receive Goods Invoice Received Match Invoice Release Payment Close Case Check PR before PO Yes Approve before PO Yes PO before Goods Invoice Yes Match before Payment Yes Payment before Close Yes Verdict Looks standard No obvious anomalies

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1002 Sequence Create PR Issue PO Invoice Received Release Payment Approve PR Receive Goods Match Invoice Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Check Major Issue Payment happened before the Purchase Requisition was approved Payment happened before Goods Received Payment before Invoice Match PO before Approval Verdict Highly anomalous Payment made before approval before goods received before matching High fraud risk

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1003 Sequence Create PR Approve PR Issue PO Release Payment Invoice Received Receive Goods Match Invoice Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Check Payment before Invoice Received Payment before Goods Received Payment before Matching Verdict Highly anomalous Paying before receiving goods or invoice violates the Three Way Match principle

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 Sequence Create PR Approve PR Receive Goods Issue PO Invoice Received Match Invoice Release Payment Close Case

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Check Receive Goods before Issue PO Note While the time gap is small logically you generally should not receive goods before there is an authorized PO This is Blind Receiving or Maverick Buying Invoice Match before Payment Yes Payment before Close Yes Verdict Anomalous Receiving before PO creates inventory financial reconciliation issues and bypasses authorization

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesize Findings Case 1001 Normal Case 1002 Multiple severe anomalies Payment before Approval Payment before Goods Payment before Match Case 1003 Severe anomaly Payment before Invoice Goods Case 1004 Moderate Significant anomaly Goods before PO

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Draft the Explanation Structure the response clearly Overview Case by Case Analysis Summary of Risks For each anomaly define the violation and the risk Financial loss Fraud Compliance Audit failure Use specific timestamps to prove the point

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Refine the Analysis Self Correction Edge Cases Case 1002 The timestamp for Approve Purchase Requisition is 0203 0800 The Release Payment was 0203 0715 So Payment happened before Approval Also Goods Received was 0204 So Payment happened before Goods Case 1003 Payment 0208 Invoice 0209 Goods 0210 This is paying for nothing Case 1004 Goods 0900 PO 0915 This is a post dated PO or receiving without authorization Case 1001 Just ensure I do not miss anything subtle 0202 Goods 0202 Invoice 0205 Match 0206 Payment This is a bit slow 3 days to match but not an anomaly in sequence

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Polish Ensure the tone is professional and analytical Highlight the specific business controls violated Segregation of Duties Authorization Three Way Match Make sure to explicitly mention the Three Way Match concept as it is central to P2P controls
